Home → Demo Hub → System Story

Plain English

The Agent Command Center is a control room before the switches are wired.

It is a production-visible, read-only dashboard that proves the readiness architecture exists before runtime activation is allowed. The system is complete through MVP-62, but live automation is intentionally disabled.

Production verified through MVP-62 plus Continual Harness Operator Mode. Full-fleet MVP-63 through MVP-68 surfaces are not yet merged into this branch. NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION Runtime activation not started

The System in One Sentence

The Agent Command Center is a production-visible control room that organizes operational readiness layers, validator gates, safety boundaries, and stakeholder-facing review materials before any real automation or execution is allowed.

What it proves

The dashboard proves the architecture is complete enough to review, audit, and present. It does not prove live execution is enabled.

What remains disabled

No commands, actions, writes, alerts, rollback execution, incident mutation, deploy controls, or automation are active.

Why it matters

Stakeholders can inspect readiness honestly before any operational risk is introduced.

System story

How the product is framed

Readiness architecture, not runtime

The dashboard documents the readiness stack instead of pretending the runtime is already enabled. That is deliberate and safer.

Visible system scale

The repo exposes verified counts for reports, validators, artifacts, layers, and the canonical registry export. The exact agent count is 47,979 and the exact department count is 1,777, both derived from 09_exports/org_chart_export.json.

Operational domains instead of departments

The docs use 11 operational domains in place of formal department names. That keeps the language precise and avoids false structure.

Validators and safety gates

Validators prove the markers, the latest status, the wall, and the package boundaries. They do not turn the system into a live execution engine.

Registry traceability

The published registry page and traceability HTML show how the counts were derived from project artifacts without inventing new runtime data.

© 2026 Devin O’Rourke. All rights reserved.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a static, read-only demonstration. It does not enable live automation, command execution, database writes, Supabase mutations, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or runtime activation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
